title: Build animated 'see the swarm' visual as a standalone worker module
description:
Build the centerpiece orchestration visual as a STANDALONE module so it can be developed in parallel with the landing.ts copy work (different file = no merge conflict). Spec: docs/promo-website-orchestration-update-roadmap.md section 4.6.

Deliverable: cloud/worker/src/swarm-visual.ts exporting e.g. 'export const swarmSection = `<section>...</section>`;' — a self-contained block (markup + inline CSS keyframes + inline SVG) using the SAME brand tokens already in landing.ts (green nodes, --surface cards, mono labels, the existing glow filter). It must NOT import from landing.ts.

What it depicts (option B, animated):
- Dependency graph: ticks as nodes, blocked_by = solid arrows, after = dashed. Use the README's auth example shape so it mirrors real 'tk graph' output (5 ticks, 3 waves, max 2 parallel; critical path label).
- Wave decomposition into columns; annotate 'max parallel' and 'critical path'.
- Parallel worktree agents: each ready tick in a wave lights up an agent lane (branch tick/<epic>/<id>) concurrently — the 'many agents at once' moment.
- Wave-by-wave integration: agents finish -> branches merge -> wave goes green -> next wave's nodes unblock and light up. Loop.

CONSTRAINTS: pure CSS/SVG animation (or tiny vanilla JS timeline) — no framework, no build step, no new deps. Honor prefers-reduced-motion with a static rendering. Keep loop-able and ideally pausable. Graph shape/labels must match real tk graph semantics.

CONTRACT for the integration tick: export name 'swarmSection' (string). VERIFY: cd cloud/worker && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit.
acceptance_criteria: New file cloud/worker/src/swarm-visual.ts exports a self-contained section (HTML+inline CSS/SVG) as a string const; animates waves->parallel-agents->merge->unblock, loops; prefers-reduced-motion shows static fallback; no external deps; cd cloud/worker && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit clean
